circuit.name: electrical circuit
search_queries: Design and analysis of electrical circuits involving resistors, capacitors, and inductors using electronics design tools and fundamental laws such as Ohm's law and Kirchhoff's law.
Electrical engineer creating an electrical schematic and netlist for analog electronics or power electronics circuits using KiCad, LTspice, or CircuiTikZ with simulation via SPICE model or PySpice.

Electrican performing voltage analysis and current analysis on an electrical prototype using a multimeter and simulation software like LTspice or EDIF, supported by tools such as KiCad and PySpice for accurate circuit design.
University professor for electrical engineering who teaches circuit design for analog electronics using SPICE netlists for electrical schematics.

electrical engineer, circuit, schematic, netlist, electronics design, capacitor, inductor, resistor, electrical schematic, analog electronics, Ohm's law, SPICE model, voltage analysis, current analysis, power electronics, Kirchhoff's law, multimeter, electrical prototyp, LTspice, KiCad, EDIF, CircuiTikZ, PySpice

spice.code_instruct: The netlist should not contain control statements or dot commands.

tikz.code_instruct: The components should be positioned correctly and orthogonally. The TikZ code should not contain comments or textual descriptions and should start with '''\begin{circuitikz}'''.

hdl.name: digital hardware design
search_queries: Digital hardware design involves creating digital systems using hardware description languages like VHDL and Verilog, focusing on register transfer level and gate-level design for FPGA and ASIC implementations.

Digital design engineer specializing in digital logic and hardware design, experienced in Verilog and VHDL, with expertise in FPGA development, logic simulation using Yosys, and verification of sequential and combinational logic circuits.

Professional in digital development working on chip design and digital circuit implementation, utilizing hardware simulation and logic gates to verify digital electronics systems for ASIC and FPGA applications.
University professor for digital systems who teaches digital hardware design for FPGAs and ASICs using hardware description languages VHDL and Verilog.

digital design engineer, hardware description language (HDL), VHDL, Verilog, register transfer level, field-programmable gate array (FPGA), application-specific integrated circuit (ASIC), chip design, flip-flop, logic gates, digital systems, hardware simulation, hardware design, digital design verification, digital logic, logic simulation, digital circuit, digital electronics, digital development, gate-level design, Yosys, sequential logic, combinational logic

vhdl.code_instruct: The code should be for a single entity.

verilog.code_instruct: The code should be for a single module.

logic.name: propositional logic
search_queries: A study of propositional logic involving logical operators, truth tables, and symbolic logic in the context of digital systems and formal logic.
Electrical Engineer designing combinational circuits using logic gates, boolean expressions, and logical connectives based on principles of propositional logic and digital logic.
Mathematicaian analyzing and simplifying boolean algebra and boolean logic in digital circuits through truth tables, logical operators, and combinational logic design.

University professor for digital systems who teaches boolean algebra and boolean logic in digital circuits through truth tables, logical expressions, logical connective symbols and combinational logic design.

logic gate, logical operator, digital circuit, digital logic, digital system, logic circuit, symbolic logic, boolean algebra, boolean expression, boolean logic, truth table, formal logic, combinational logic, combinational circuit, logical connective

logic_bool.code_instruct: The expressions should use operators ('and', 'or', 'nand', 'nor', 'xor', 'xnor', 'not') in one single formula without '=' and, IMPORTANTLY, the code should consist of only one single line and the operators should be between the variables/parentheses, not at the beginning.

logic_symb.code_instruct: The symbols ('¬', '∧', '∨', '⊻') should be in one single formula without '=' and, IMPORTANTLY, the code should consist of only one single line.

plc.name: programmable logic controller (PLC)
search_queries: A control engineer working with programmable logic controller (PLC) systems who uses ladder logic diagram (LD), function block diagram (FBD), structured text (ST), instruction list (IL), and sequential function charts (SFC) for industrial control and automation design in compliance with IEC 61131 standards, integrating OPC UA and AutomationML for factory automation and control engineering applications.

Electrical Engineer specializing in industrial automation and control systems, utilizing programmable logic controller (PLC) programming languages including structured text (ST), ladder logic diagram (LD), and function block diagram (FBD), with experience in CODESYS, OPC UA communication, and AutomationML for automation design in manufacturing environments.

Expert in control engineering and industrial control systems who develops and implements PLC-based automation solutions using IEC 61131-3 programming methods such as instruction list (IL), sequential function charts (SFC), and CODESYS platform, supporting factory automation through integration of OPC UA and AutomationML standards.

University professor for control engineering who teaches industrial automation systems using programmable logic controller (PLC) programming languages including structured text (ST), ladder logic diagram (LD), and function block diagram (FBD).

programmable logic controller (PLC), ladder logic diagram (LD), function block diagram (FBD), structured text (ST), instruction list (IL), sequential function charts (SFC), industrial control, industrial automation, factory automation, control engineering, control system, automation design, IEC 61131, OPC UA, AutomationML, CODESYS

il.code_instruct:
The code should contain variable definitions and only the commands LD, LDN, AND, OR, XOR, ANDN, ORN, XORN, NOT, ST, STN, S or R and no jump or math commands should be used. The code should start with '''VAR
    '''.

quantum.name: quantum circuit
search_queries: A description of a quantum circuit involving qubits, superposition, and entanglement using frameworks like Qiskit, Cirq, and QASM with gate array design in superconducting systems.
Electrical Engineer designing quantum circuits using Qiskit and Cirq, leveraging superposition and entanglement in superconducting qubits, and writing quantum programs in QASM and QCircuit.

Physicist experienced in implementing quantum circuits through gate array architectures, utilizing superposition and entanglement principles, and working with tools like QASM, Cirq, Qiskit, and QCircuit on superconducting qubit platforms.
University professor for physics and electrical engineering who teaches quantum circuit design involving qubits and gate array architectures using frameworks like Qiskit, Cirq, and QASM.
quantum circuit, superposition, entanglement, Qubit, gate array, superconducting, QASM, Cirq, QCircuit, Qiskit

tree.name: phylogenetic tree
search_queries: A phylogenetic tree is used in evolutionary biology to represent the evolutionary relationships among species, showing common ancestry and divergence across fields like paleontology, herpetology, entomology, ornithology, ichthyology, plant biology, mycology, and horticulture through the application of cladistics and cladograms.
Researcher specialized in cladistics who constructs phylogenetic trees and cladograms to study ancestral relationships across species in horticulture, ornithology, and evolutionary biology.

Analyzing species evolution through phylogenetic trees and cladograms based on shared common ancestors, utilizing methods from cladistics and research in evolutionary biology, paleontology, and organismal studies including plant biology, horticulture, and various zoological disciplines.
University professor for evolutionary theory and biology teaching phylogenetic trees and cladograms for species in fields like ornithology, ichthyology, paleontology, herpetology, and entomology.

phylogenetic tree, paleontology, herpetology, entomology, ornithology, horticulture, ichthyology, evolutionary biology, plant biology, mycology, cladistics, cladogram, common ancestor, species, ancestral

newick.code_instruct: The code should start with '(' and, IMPORTANTLY, the code should consist of only one single line.

phyloxml.code_instruct:
The code should start with '''<phyloxml xmlns="http://www.phyloxml.org">
  <phylogeny rooted="true">
    <clade>'''.

dna.name: DNA sequence
search_queries: Deoxyribonucleic acid (DNA) and ribonucleic acid (RNA) are central to genetic information storage and transfer, involving nucleotide sequences, base pairing, genome structure, and gene expression, with analysis tools like ViennaRNA using Dot-bracket notation and FASTA format processed via Biopython to study mutations and base sequences.

Biological researcher who uses FASTA sequences, Biopython, and ViennaRNA for genome sequencing, base pair alignment, gene expression patterns, and mutation detection, with expertise in Dot-bracket notation for RNA secondary structure prediction.

Biologist as a proficient in handling genetic data with expertise in nucleotide sequence analysis, genome annotation, detecting mutations, interpreting base sequences, and utilizing ViennaRNA with Dot-bracket notation for RNA secondary structure prediction, alongside processing FASTA sequences using Biopython for gene expression studies.

University professor for biology teaching genetics involving nucleotide sequences of Deoxyribonucleic acid (DNA) and ribonucleic acid (RNA) using FASTA sequences, Biopython, and ViennaRNA for gene expressions.
deoxyribonucleic acid (DNA), ribonucleic acid (RNA), genetic, nucleotide, genome, base pair, base sequence, gene expression, mutations, ViennaRNA, Dot-bracket notation, FASTA sequence, Biopython

fasta.code_instruct: The code should contain a single FASTA sequence or multiple different sequences of same length (length less than 30). The code should start with '>'.

vienna.code_instruct: The code should be in Vienna RNA format for secondary structures containing a sequence and corresponding dot-bracket notation with length less than 100. The dot-bracket notation should be on the line after the sequence and, importantly, be exactly the same length as the sequence. The code should start with '>'.

mol.name: molecule or chemical structure
terms_semantic: Simplified Molecular Input Line Entry System (SMILES)
search_queries: Using computational cheminformatics tools like RDKit to interpret molecular data formats including SMILES, SMARTS, Molfile, and PDB for analyzing reaction mechanisms, atomic interactions, and the chemical structure of molecules.
Chemical reasearcher who specializes in interpreting atomic-level details of molecules, including Lewis structures, crystal structures, and reaction mechanisms.

Chemist working with molecular representations such as Lewis structure, crystal structure, structural formula, and using SMILES, SMARTS, Molfile, PDB, and RDKit to study reaction mechanisms and atomic-level chemical structure.
University professor for chemistry teaching molecular and atomic structures, such as Lewis structures, crystal structures, and reaction mechanisms using data formats including SMILES and SMARTS.

molecules, chemical structure, atomic, chemist, lewis structure, crystal structure, structural formula, reaction mechanisms, Simplified Molecular Input Line Entry System (SMILES), SMARTS, Molfile, Protein Data Bank (PDB), RDKit

smarts.code_instruct: The code should be in SMARTS with multiple elements and, IMPORTANTLY, the code should consist of only one single line.

smarts_react.code_instruct: The code should contain '>>' for separating reactants and products in the reaction. IMPORTANTLY, the code should consist of only one single line.

cml.code_instruct: The code should start with '''<cml xmlns="http://www.xml-cml.org/schema"'''.
